claim_natural: The Goldbach conjecture holds for every even integer greater than 2.

claim_formal.subject: Goldbach conjecture
claim_formal.operator_note: The Goldbach conjecture asserts that every even integer > 2 is the sum of two primes. This is an unresolved conjecture — no proof or disproof exists as of 2026. Computational verification up to a finite bound provides evidence but cannot prove the claim for all even integers. The literature records verification up to 4 x 10^18 (Oliveira e Silva, 2013). Our script verifies up to 10^6 with two independent methods. Verdict is always UNDETERMINED regardless of computational outcome.

A1.label: Primary check: counterexamples in [4, 10^6] via trial division

A2.label: Cross-check: counterexamples in [4, 10^6] via prime-pair sieve

cross_checks.description: Trial division vs prime sieve methods
cross_checks.independence: Mathematically independent: Method 1 uses per-number isprime() calls; Method 2 pre-generates a prime set via sieve and uses set membership. Different algorithms, different data structures.

adversarial_checks.question: Has any counterexample to Goldbach's conjecture ever been found?
adversarial_checks.verification_performed: Searched: 'Goldbach conjecture counterexample found disproved'. Reviewed Wikipedia, Physics Forums, Medium articles, and LessWrong. No counterexample has ever been reported.
adversarial_checks.finding: No counterexample exists in the literature or computational records.

adversarial_checks.question: Has the Goldbach conjecture been formally proved or disproved?
adversarial_checks.verification_performed: Searched: 'Goldbach conjecture proof solved 2024 2025 2026'. Found several claimed proofs in non-peer-reviewed venues (SSRN, SCIRP, preprints.org, ScienceOpen) but none accepted by the mainstream mathematical community. The conjecture remains open as of March 2026.
adversarial_checks.finding: No universally accepted proof or disproof exists. The conjecture is listed as an open problem by all major references.

adversarial_checks.question: What is the current computational verification bound?
adversarial_checks.verification_performed: Searched: 'Goldbach conjecture verified computational bound 2026'. Oliveira e Silva verified up to 4 x 10^18 (2013). The Gridbach project (2025) extended this further. A March 2025 preprint reports empirical verification beyond 4 quintillion.
adversarial_checks.finding: Verified computationally up to at least 4 x 10^18. Our 10^6 bound is far below the literature record but uses two independent methods.

verdict_reason: No counterexamples found up to 1000000 by two independent methods. However, this is a universal conjecture over infinitely many even integers. Computational verification of a finite range cannot constitute a proof. The Goldbach conjecture remains an open problem.
